proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_cetuximab have a different mean pfs_months than those not receiving treatment_cetuximab.
Patients receiving treatment_bevacizumab have a different mean pfs_months than those not receiving treatment_bevacizumab.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a different mean pfs_months than those not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.
Patients receiving treatment_encorafenib have a different mean pfs_months than those not receiving treatment_encorafenib.
Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib have a different mean pfs_months than those not receiving treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib.
Patients receiving treatment_regorafenib have higher mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 4.285 on cetuximab vs 4.323 off (n_on=15,078); two-sample t-test p=0.089. No significant main effect.
Mean pfs_months 4.301 on bevacizumab vs 4.320 off (n_on=22,383); t-test p=0.354. No significant main effect.
Mean pfs_months 4.316 on pembrolizumab vs 4.311 off (n_on=7,660); t-test p=0.855. No significant main effect.
Mean pfs_months 4.316 on encorafenib vs 4.311 off (n_on=5,111); t-test p=0.873. No significant main effect.
Mean pfs_months 4.271 on tras/tuc vs 4.315 off (n_on=4,033); t-test p=0.246. No significant main effect.
Mean pfs_months 5.089 on regorafenib vs 4.117 off (n_on=10,022); t-test p<1e-300. Strongly higher pfs_months among regorafenib recipients (+0.97 months).

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_cetuximab benefit on pfs_months is larger (more positive) in kras_mutation==0 patients than in kras_mutation==1 patients (the established anti-EGFR/RAS-resistance pattern).
treatment_cetuximab benefit on pfs_months is larger in nras_mutation==0 than in nras_mutation==1 patients.
treatment_cetuximab benefit on pfs_months is larger in braf_v600e==0 than in braf_v600e==1 patients.
treatment_cetuximab benefit on pfs_months is larger in left-sided (right_sided_primary==0) than in right-sided primaries.
treatment_pembrolizumab benefit on pfs_months is larger in msi_high==1 patients than in msi_high==0 patients (the established immune-checkpoint/MSI-high pattern).
treatment_encorafenib benefit on pfs_months is larger in braf_v600e==1 patients than in braf_v600e==0 patients.
treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib benefit on pfs_months is larger in her2_amplified==1 patients than in her2_amplified==0 patients.
treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months differs by kras_mutation status.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term treatment_cetuximab:kras_mutation beta=+0.020, p=0.665. No significant interaction; the expected RAS-WT-restricted benefit is NOT present.
Interaction beta=-0.007, p=0.96. No significant cetuximab × NRAS interaction.
Interaction beta=-0.026, p=0.805. No significant cetuximab × BRAF V600E interaction.
Interaction beta=+0.026, p=0.583. No significant cetuximab × sidedness interaction.
Interaction beta=+0.002, p=0.988. The hallmark pembrolizumab/MSI-high benefit is NOT detectable in this dataset.
Interaction beta=-0.141, p=0.376. Encorafenib provides no detectable extra PFS benefit in BRAF V600E patients.
Interaction beta=+0.063, p=0.778. Trastuzumab/tucatinib provides no detectable extra PFS benefit in HER2-amplified patients.
Interaction beta=-1.629, p=7.7e-226. Regorafenib effect is hugely modified by KRAS: benefit is concentrated in KRAS WT patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within kras_mutation==0, treatment_regorafenib increases mean pfs_months substantially; within kras_mutation==1, treatment_regorafenib has little or no effect on pfs_months.
treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months is also modified by nras_mutation status (benefit largest in NRAS WT).
treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months is modified by braf_v600e status (benefit largest in BRAF WT).

analyses.result_summary: KRAS WT (n=29,102): regorafenib +1.656 mo (5.773 vs 4.117, p<1e-300). KRAS mut (n=20,898): regorafenib +0.027 mo (4.143 vs 4.116, p=0.45). Confirms KRAS-WT-restricted regorafenib benefit.
treatment_regorafenib:nras_mutation beta=+0.065, p=0.26. NRAS does not significantly modify regorafenib effect in adjusted model.
treatment_regorafenib:braf_v600e beta=-1.747, p=5.3e-285. BRAF V600E essentially abolishes the regorafenib benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months is modified by right_sided_primary: benefit is concentrated in left-sided primaries (right_sided_primary==0).
There is a three-way interaction such that the regorafenib PFS benefit is restricted to left-sided AND kras_mutation==0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction right_sided_primary:treatment_regorafenib beta=-1.434, p<1e-300. Regorafenib helps left-sided primaries; benefit is roughly nil in right-sided primaries.

Mean pfs_months in left/KRAS WT/regorafenib = 6.64 vs 4.12 off (n=3,763 vs 15,090; +2.52 mo). Other strata: regorafenib effect ≈ 0. Three-way interaction term beta=+2.49, p<1e-300, restoring near-zero effect outside the left+KRAS-WT cell.

proposed_hypotheses.text: stage_iv==1 patients have lower mean pfs_months than stage_iv==0.
right_sided_primary==1 patients have lower mean pfs_months than left-sided.
kras_mutation==1 patients have lower mean pfs_months than kras_mutation==0.
braf_v600e==1 patients have lower mean pfs_months than braf_v600e==0.
nras_mutation==1 patients have different mean pfs_months than nras_mutation==0.
msi_high==1 patients have different mean pfs_months than msi_high==0.
her2_amplified==1 patients have different mean pfs_months than her2_amplified==0.
liver_mets==1 patients have lower mean pfs_months than liver_mets==0.
tp53_mutation==1 patients have different mean pfs_months than tp53_mutation==0.

analyses.result_summary: Right-sided 4.114 vs left 4.419, diff=-0.305, p=5.2e-46.
NRAS mut 4.526 vs WT 4.305, diff=+0.221, p=2.3e-04. NRAS-mutant patients have slightly HIGHER PFS (small effect).
MSI-high 4.293 vs MSS 4.313, diff=-0.019, p=0.682. No prognostic difference.
HER2 amp 4.258 vs non-amp 4.313, diff=-0.056, p=0.354. No prognostic difference.
Liver mets 4.296 vs none 4.314, diff=-0.019, p=0.516. No detectable PFS difference.
TP53 mut 4.301 vs WT 4.320, diff=-0.018, p=0.375. No detectable difference.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher age_years is associated with lower mean pfs_months (typical clinical expectation).
Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower pfs_months.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher pfs_months.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower pfs_months.
Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with lower pfs_months.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower pfs_months.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower pfs_months.
Higher nlr is associated with lower pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: r=+0.766, p<1e-300. STRIKINGLY positive: older patients have LONGER PFS — opposite of typical clinical pattern.
r=-0.352, p<1e-300. Higher ECOG → lower PFS, as expected.
r=+0.102, p=3.3e-115. Higher albumin → longer PFS, as expected.
r=-0.123, p=2.2e-167. Higher weight loss → lower PFS, as expected.
r=-0.038, p=3.1e-17. Small but significant inverse association.
r=-0.011, p=0.013. Tiny inverse correlation, statistically significant only because of large n.
r=-0.008, p=0.065. Not significant.
r=+0.005, p=0.29. Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for ecog_ps, stage_iv, albumin_g_dl, and weight_loss_pct_6mo, higher age_years is still associated with HIGHER pfs_months (i.e. the unusual age direction is not explained by case-mix).
There is a significant age_years × ecog_ps interaction on pfs_months: PFS decline with worsening performance status is steeper at older ages.
There is an age_years × stage_iv interaction on pfs_months: stage IV PFS penalty is larger at older ages.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted age_years coefficient = +0.176 mo per year of age (p<1e-300). Direction unchanged after case-mix adjustment.
age_years:ecog_ps beta=-0.0069, p=1.5e-23. Combined high age + high ECOG worsens PFS more than sum of main effects.
age_years:stage_iv beta=-0.0066, p=2.7e-12. Stage IV penalty is steeper at older ages, but main age effect remains positive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs between sex_female==1 and sex_female==0 patients.
Mean pfs_months differs across race_ethnicity groups.
Mean pfs_months differs across insurance_type categories (uninsured worst).
Patients with rural_residence==1 have different pfs_months than those with rural_residence==0.
smoking_pack_years is associated with pfs_months.
education_years is associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Female 4.308 vs male 4.314, diff=-0.006, p=0.77. No sex disparity.
Group means 4.273 (black) - 4.407 (other); ANOVA F=0.87, p=0.479. No significant difference across racial/ethnic groups.
Adjusted differences vs medicaid: medicare +0.002 (p=0.88), private +0.006 (p=0.69), uninsured -0.046 (p=0.084). No significant disparity.
Rural 4.331 vs urban 4.307, diff=+0.024, p=0.338. No significant difference.
r=+0.0005, p=0.913. No association.
r=+0.0043, p=0.337. No association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Common comorbidities (diabetes_mellitus, hypertension, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure, coronary_artery_disease, atrial_fibrillation, depression_anxiety_diagnosis) are each associated with lower pfs_months.
Prior anti-cancer therapies (prior_chemotherapy, prior_radiation, prior_surgery, prior_immunotherapy, prior_targeted_therapy) are each associated with different pfs_months.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind for each binary comorbidity vs pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: All comorbidities tested showed |diff| < 0.08 mo and p > 0.05 (e.g., diabetes_mellitus diff=-0.013 p=0.61; depression_anxiety_diagnosis diff=-0.052 p=0.053; atrial_fibrillation diff=-0.059 p=0.119). No comorbidity is a clinically meaningful prognostic factor in this cohort.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind for each prior_* covariate vs pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: All effects |diff| < 0.04 mo and p > 0.15 (e.g., prior_chemotherapy diff=-0.005 p=0.82; prior_immunotherapy diff=-0.034 p=0.23). Prior treatment history does not predict PFS in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Of the 27 snp_* variables, at least one shows a significant main effect on pfs_months after Bonferroni correction (alpha=0.05/27=0.00185).
At least one snp_* variable significantly modifies the treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months at Bonferroni alpha=0.05/27.

analyses.code: for each snp: stats.ttest_ind on pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: Smallest p was snp_rs1801131 (p=0.027) and snp_rs1050828 (p=0.015); none survives Bonferroni (alpha=0.00185). No SNP main effect on PFS.

Three SNPs cross Bonferroni: snp_rs1800629 interaction +0.126 (p=7.7e-06), snp_rs2070744 interaction -0.070 (p=1.5e-03), snp_rs1801197 interaction -0.097 (p=2.6e-04). Effects are small in absolute magnitude (<0.13 mo) but statistically robust modifiers of regorafenib benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the canonical 'optimal' subgroup of left-sided RAS-WT patients (right_sided_primary==0 AND kras_mutation==0 AND nras_mutation==0), treatment_cetuximab improves pfs_months compared to no cetuximab.
Within braf_v600e==1 patients, treatment_encorafenib improves pfs_months after adjustment for age, ECOG, and stage.
Within msi_high==1 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months after adjustment for age, ECOG, and stage.

analyses.result_summary: Left RAS WT subgroup n=17,924; cetuximab on (n=5,510) mean 4.574 vs off (n=12,414) mean 4.635; diff=-0.062, p=0.138. Cetuximab does NOT improve PFS even in the canonical favorable subset.
treatment_encorafenib beta=+0.002, p=0.935 in BRAF V600E patients. No PFS benefit. (Pembrolizumab in BRAF subset borderline +0.048, p=0.061.)
treatment_pembrolizumab beta=+0.034, p=0.541 in MSI-high patients. No PFS benefit. (Bevacizumab in MSI-high subset nominal +0.096, p=0.019.)

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving BOTH treatment_cetuximab AND treatment_bevacizumab have different pfs_months than those receiving neither/only one (potential negative interaction, mirroring CALGB/SWOG 80405-era observations).
Patients receiving BOTH treatment_encorafenib AND treatment_cetuximab have different pfs_months than those without this combination.

analyses.result_summary: Cetux+Bev (n=6,772) mean 4.254 vs others 4.321; diff=-0.067, p=0.025. Small statistically significant lower PFS in dual-recipients (likely confounded by co-treatment selection).
Enco+Cetux (n=1,495) mean 4.231 vs others 4.314; diff=-0.083, p=0.17. Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS for pfs_months including age_years, sex_female, ecog_ps, stage_iv, right_sided_primary, kras_mutation, nras_mutation, braf_v600e, msi_high, her2_amplified, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, cea_ng_ml, ldh_u_l, all six treatment indicators, liver_mets, bone_mets, tp53_mutation, and pik3ca_mutation, treatment_regorafenib retains a positive coefficient while the other treatment indicators have null coefficients.
Even after adjusting for case-mix and biomarkers, the three-way treatment_regorafenib × kras_mutation × right_sided_primary interaction term is highly significant.

analyses.result_summary: R^2=0.858. Significant adjusted coefficients: age_years +0.176 (p<1e-300), ecog_ps -1.171, stage_iv -1.361, right_sided_primary -0.287, kras_mutation -0.354, braf_v600e -0.355, albumin_g_dl +0.470, weight_loss_pct_6mo -0.076, cea_ng_ml -0.005, treatment_regorafenib +0.943 (p<1e-300). Other treatments and msi_high/her2_amplified/tp53/pik3ca are non-significant.

Three-way term beta=+2.516, p<1e-300; main regorafenib term +2.517 (left/KRAS WT effect); two-way regorafenib:right beta=-2.522 and regorafenib:kras beta=-2.516. Adjusted R^2=0.907. Confirms regorafenib benefit is precisely localized to left-sided RAS-WT patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: race_ethnicity modifies the treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months (e.g., disparity by race in benefit).
sex_female modifies the treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months.
stage_iv modifies the treatment_regorafenib effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Stratified regorafenib effect (adjusted, with KRAS interaction): white +1.59 (p<1e-300), hispanic +1.64, asian +1.60, black +1.72, other +1.76. Pooled C(race_ethnicity):treatment_regorafenib interaction p>=0.15 for all categories. No racial heterogeneity in regorafenib benefit.
sex_female:treatment_regorafenib beta=+0.028, p=0.20. No sex modification.
stage_iv:treatment_regorafenib beta=+0.029, p=0.18. Not significant; regorafenib benefit roughly uniform across stage IV vs non-stage IV.
